title: [ExportVerilog] Expressions in sub-blocks do not get emitted out-of-line appropriately
body:
If you have an expression which cannot be inlined in a sub-block (e.g. `AlwaysOp`), it will be inlined inappropriately. The problem is that [`collectNamesEmitDecls`](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/8cbe578d2363a9e97395d1b7af99d9ad3cac60bc/lib/Translation/ExportVerilog/ExportVerilog.cpp#L1656) is only run on the top level module.

I'm not sure the best way to fix this. We could run `collectNamesEmitDecls` recursively but that would declare wires unnecessarily at the module scope. We could run it in `ModuleEmitter::visitSV(<block ops>)` but that would use the module-level name collision detection, so it will pollute the module namespace. We could create a hierarchical name collision resolution, but I don't think that's worth the effort at this point.
